rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE EDWARD H. HELLER TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records
rulings.Rulings: Respondent Olivia Mandell's motion to enforce the deposition subpoena for production of business records from Wells Fargo Bank is denied for failure to comply with the requirements of Code of Civil Procedure Section 1985.3(b). Upon re-issuance of the subpoena, counsel for respondent shall meet and confer with counsel for Wells Fargo Bank, either in person or telephonically, to address the issue of the proper scope of the request. (610/BCHAN)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE EDWARD H. HELLER TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Answers To Form Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Respondent Olivia Mandell's motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories is continued to December 6, 2004 at 9:00 a.m. Counsel for respondent is ordered to comply with Local Rule 8.5 (A) and provide courtesy copies of the moving parties in advance of the hearing. The parties are ordered to make a reasonable and good faith attempt to resolve the items in dispute. (610/BCHAN)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE EDWARD H. HELLER TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: To Enforce Deposition Subpoena
rulings.Rulings: Respondent Olivia Mandell's motion to enforce deposition subpoena -- Duplicate Entry.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE EDWARD H. HELLER TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Response To Demand For Production
rulings.Rulings: Respondent Olivia Mandell's motion to compel further responses to request for production #2 and #3 is denied. The request for monetary sanctions by the Heller trustees and beneficiaries is granted. Counsel for the trustees and beneficiaries shall submit a supplemental declaration in support of the request for sanctions set forth in the opposition to the motion. The court finds that by filing the instant motion, counsel for respondent employed a discovery method in a manner that caused undue burden and expense. The documents that were the subject of the previous order were available for inspection on September 21, 2004. Counsel for respondent thereafter failed to respond to several requests to execute a confidentiality agreement before copying of the documents occurred. Counsel for respondent did not make a reasonable and good faith attempt to informally resolve the matter, but instead filed the instant motion. (610/BCHAN)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE EDWARD H. HELLER TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Imposing Sanctions For Failure To Obey Prior Court Orders
rulings.Rulings: Respondent Olivia Mandell's motion to impose issue and monetary sanctions for violation of court order is denied. (610/BCHAN)
